title: Kraken Seeks European Banking License in Lithuania
summary: Kraken, a major crypto exchange, is applying for a banking license in Lithuania to expand its financial services. This move could allow Kraken to offer traditional banking products alongside its crypto services.
body:
Kraken, one of the world's largest cryptocurrency exchanges, is making a significant move into traditional banking. The company is seeking a banking license in Lithuania, according to a person familiar with the plans.

Lithuania has emerged as a hub for crypto-friendly financial services, making it an attractive jurisdiction for Kraken. Securing a banking license would enable Kraken to offer services such as savings accounts, loans, and payment processing, in addition to its existing crypto trading platform.

For everyday users, this could mean more integrated financial services. Imagine being able to manage both your crypto investments and traditional bank accounts in one place. This could simplify financial management and provide more options for users who want to bridge the gap between traditional and digital finance.

Kraken is not the first crypto exchange to explore banking services. Other exchanges like Binance and Coinbase have also shown interest in expanding into traditional banking. However, Kraken's move into Lithuania could set a precedent for how crypto exchanges operate within the European financial landscape.

If successful, Kraken's banking license could be a game-changer. Keep an eye on regulatory approvals and any new product announcements from Kraken in the coming months.
twitterThread: Kraken is trying to become a bank in Europe, targeting Lithuania for its license. This could mean traditional banking services like loans and savings accounts on a crypto platform.
By securing a banking license, Kraken aims to offer more integrated financial services. Lithuania's crypto-friendly regulations make it an attractive choice for this expansion.

If approved, Kraken's banking services could simplify financial management for users. Watch for regulatory updates and new product announcements → https://cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-07-07-kraken-seeks-european-banking-license-in-lithuania
